title: If Bruins are looking to make a deal, the Ducks are an appealing trade partner
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins eye Ducks' Olen Zellweger and Mason McTavish as potential trade targets to enhance speed and skill.
Anaheim's cap space and pending contracts may prompt them to consider trading assets, including younger players.
Zellweger and McTavish could fit Bruins' need for defensive upgrades and top-six center talent.

title: Shark season is here. One just popped up in a Rhode Island pond.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A porbeagle shark was found in Point Judith Pond, Rhode Island, and later beached and died.
The Atlantic Shark Institute reported the shark sightings began near Salty Brine State Beach in Narragansett.
NOAA conducted a necropsy on the shark, which posed no danger to people according to experts.

title: Families continue to fight BPS exam school admissions policy after judge rules no discrimination against white, Asian students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Boston Parent Coalition appeals a dismissed case, claiming BPS exam school admissions policies discriminate by using socio-economic tiers as race proxies.
A federal judge previously dismissed the coalition's claims, stating the tiered admission doesn't violate students' equal protection under the Fourteenth Amendment.
Boston Public Schools maintains its policies are lawful, aiming for equitable access and strong learning opportunities for high-achieving students.

title: Patriots receivers well aware of the A.J. Brown trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown is rumored to be traded from the Eagles to the Patriots after June 1 due to salary cap considerations.
Patriots receiver Kyle Williams and teammates are aware of trade rumors but focus on improving their skills, acknowledging Brown's talent.
Veteran safety Kevin Byard praised Brown's impact on opposing defenses, noting his role in dictating coverage and winning matchups.

title: Students in Maine were accidentally served dirt for dessert, school says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Three students at Medomak Valley High School in Maine accidentally ate dirt, mistaking it for dessert.
The dirt was originally used for a science project and mistakenly included in a school event meal.
School officials confirmed the mix-up was not a prank and contacted the students' parents.

title: What the Patriots are saying about Super Bowl rematch with Seahawks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots set to face Seahawks in 2026 season opener on Sept. 3, a rematch of last Super Bowl.
Safety Craig Woodson and cornerback Marcus Jones express determination to change last year's Super Bowl result.
Kenneth Walker III, Super Bowl MVP, now with Kansas City; Patriots consider matchup an opportunity to showcase growth.

title: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ review: A light, low-stakes ‘Star Wars’ movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' aims to balance large-scale 'Star Wars' themes with the series' focused storytelling.
Mando, played by Pedro Pascal, works for a New Republic leader and faces off with the Hutt twins.
The film features standout IMAX scenes but blends extensive CGI and familiar references for a low-stakes experience.

title: Patriots’ Craig Woodson is eager to build on strong rookie season ‘I’ve got to prove myself again’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Craig Woodson aims to build on a strong rookie season with the Patriots, focusing on adding skills and confidence.
Woodson became the first Patriots rookie to recover a fumble and make an interception in a playoff game last season.
Woodson has switched from jersey No. 31 to No. 4 after All-Pro Kevin Byard joined the Patriots in the offseason.

title: 2 of the most welcoming towns in the U.S. are in New England, according to World Atlas
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Burlington, Vermont, and Bar Harbor, Maine, named among 12 most welcoming U.S. towns by World Atlas.
Burlington features community events like Summer in the City and the Festival of Fools in its picturesque downtown.
Bar Harbor offers events such as the Bar Harbor Music Festival and the Dawnland Festival of Arts & Ideas.

title: Canadian man living in Saugus charged with illegally voting since 2008
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sunny Manhertz, a Canadian living in Saugus, is charged with illegal voting in federal elections since 2008.
Prosecutors allege Manhertz voted in 2012, 2016, 2020, and 2024 and submitted a false voter registration form.
If convicted, Manhertz faces up to 5 years in prison and fines up to $250,000 for casting fraudulent ballots.
